description: Standard test: health policy article with mixed statistics

statistics: "Obesity rates among adults have risen 15 percent since 2010." (Source: National Health Survey)
"People who exercise daily are 50% less likely to develop heart disease." (Source: a leading medical journal)
"The government spends $190 billion annually on obesity-related healthcare." (Source: Health Policy Institute)
"One in three children is now classified as overweight." (Source: World Health Organization)
input.story_context: Feature article on government inaction on obesity prevention policies

description: Minimal test: single statistic with limited context

statistics: "Unemployment fell to 3.2 percent last quarter." (Source: not attributed)
"Net migration to the EU increased by 42 percent in 2025." (Source: Eurostat)
"Germany alone accepted 1.2 million asylum applications last year." (Source: BAMF)
"Migrants contribute €150 billion annually to the EU economy." (Source: a think tank report)
"78 percent of irregular border crossings occur at the Mediterranean route." (Source: Frontex)
input.story_context: Investigative feature on EU migration policy reform
